raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding American Expresss handling of a dispute for goods not as described / merchandise not received ( partial ). American Express reversed my dispute based on false and unsupported claims provided by an overseas merchant, despite clear evidence contradicting the merchants statements. 

I paid the merchant in XX/XX/XXXX for a complete XXXX XXXX body kit. When the shipment arrived, it was materially incomplete, missing critical components, including the rear wing bracket and the rear bumper, which renders the product unusable. These components were part of the agreed-upon purchase and are required for installation. 

I provided American Express with extensive evidence, including : Written communication showing the merchant repeatedly failed to deliver the missing components Proof that no tracking or shipment confirmation was ever provided Documentation that the merchant coerced payment by threatening to keep my {$6000.00} deposit and resell the kit A 21-minute unboxing video ( with a direct link ) showing the shipment being opened in real time and clearly proving the missing components Despite this, American Express reversed the dispute after accepting the merchants claim that they will still produce and ship the missing items. This claim is false. As of nearly XX/XX/XXXX, more than nine months after payment, the merchant has still not delivered the missing components, has not provided proof of completion, and has not provided shipping information. American Express relied on speculative future promises rather than actual delivery or evidence. 

This reversal has caused financial harm and leaves me responsible for charges related to incomplete and unusable merchandise. I believe American Express failed to properly evaluate the evidence, relied on inaccurate merchant representations, and did not adequately protect me as a cardholder under their dispute resolution obligations. 

I am requesting that American Express be required to reopen and properly review this dispute, consider the full timeline and evidence, and reverse the charges associated with the incomplete portion of the transaction.
